tEn: Anthropic's Super Bowl Ads: A Blue Shell Attack on OpenAI?
tZh: Anthropic的超级碗广告：针对OpenAI的蓝龟壳攻击？
sEn: TVPN hosts discuss Anthropic's controversial Super Bowl ads, comparing them to political attack ads and Mario Kart's blue shell, while previewing interviews with Sam Altman and Dan Barkello.
sZh: TVPN主持人讨论Anthropic有争议的超级碗广告，将其比作政治攻击广告和马里奥赛车的蓝龟壳，同时预告了对Sam Altman和Dan Barkello的采访。

consensus.en: AI models now have massive capability overhang; better tools and orchestration matter more than raw intelligence for now.
consensus.zh: AI 模型现在有巨大的能力过剩；更好的工具和编排比原始智能更重要。

consensus.en: The future of work involves managing teams of AI agents, operating at higher levels of abstraction.
consensus.zh: 未来的工作涉及管理 AI 智能体团队，在更高的抽象层次上操作。

consensus.en: Compute power is the new oil; chip supply is the current bottleneck for AI progress.
consensus.zh: 算力是新的石油；芯片供应是当前 AI 进展的瓶颈。

consensus.en: Advertising is a proven model to scale consumer AI access; subscriptions alone limit reach.
consensus.zh: 广告是规模化消费者 AI 访问的成熟模式；仅靠订阅会限制覆盖范围。

consensus.en: Software is not dead but transforming; every company is now an API company due to agent integration.
consensus.zh: 软件没有消亡，而是在转型；由于智能体集成，每家公司现在都是 API 公司。

consensus.en: Long task horizon benchmarks like METR are key, but agent swarms may require new metrics like GDP impact.
consensus.zh: 像 METR 这样的长任务时间基准很关键，但智能体集群可能需要新的指标，如 GDP 影响。

contrarian.en: Anthropic's Super Bowl ads are deceptive political attack ads, not truthful competitive marketing.
contrarian.zh: Anthropic 的超级碗广告是欺骗性的政治攻击广告，而非诚实的竞争营销。

contrarian.en: AI-generated video is not compelling for passive viewing; its main use is personalized memes in group chats.
contrarian.zh: AI 生成的视频对被动观看没有吸引力；其主要用途是群聊中的个性化表情包。

contrarian.en: The race to 3 billion monthly active users is over; Anthropic should focus on a premium iPhone-like niche.
contrarian.zh: 争夺 30 亿月活用户的竞赛已经结束；Anthropic 应专注于类似 iPhone 的高端利基市场。

contrarian.en: World models are not on the critical path to AGI; coding and AI research are the direct route.
contrarian.zh: 世界模型不在通往 AGI 的关键路径上；编程和 AI 研究是直接路线。

contrarian.en: A software-only singularity is possible where digital tasks advance rapidly while physical world lags.
contrarian.zh: 可能出现仅软件的奇点，数字任务快速进步而物理世界滞后。

contrarian.en: Space data centers will not provide meaningful compute for OpenAI in the next 10 years.
contrarian.zh: 太空数据中心在未来 10 年内不会为 OpenAI 提供有意义的算力。

turns.en: I see a large IPO on the horizon. You're surrounded by journalists. Overnight success the place of 3000 double right. Let's miss information. Courtney declaring order inbound. Let's just roll. We are surrounded by general. Hold your position. Come get up. Trust the experts. Buy token. We are experts. Founder mode. I see multiple journalists on the horizon. Stand by. UAV online. Blaze. Double blaze. Triple blaze. Double kill. Wrong. Cop is please. Team Deathmatch. We are experts. Triple blade. Let's just roll. Right. Marky clearing order inbound. Come on, get up. We are surrounded by journalists. Hold your position. Break one. Strike two. Activate golden retriever mode. Marky clearing order inbound. 5. I see multiple journalists on the horizon. Stand by. Founder. You're watching TVPN. Today is Thursday, February 5th, 2026. We are live from the TVPN Ultradome. Yahoo! The temple of technology, the fortress of finance. Let me tell you about ramp.com. Time is money. Save both. Easy. Use corporate cards, bill pay, accounting, and a whole lot more all in one place. We have quite the lineup today. Let's pull up the linear lineup. Meet the system for modern software development. 70% of enterprises on enterprise workspaces on linear are using agents and we have a lineup for you today. We got Sam Altman from OpenAI joining Sholto from... if you're not familiar, Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI. They are the makers of ChatGPT.
turns.zh: 我看到地平线上有一场大型IPO。你被记者包围了。一夜成名，3000双倍右。别错过信息。Courtney宣布命令进场。我们开始吧。我们被将军包围了。守住你的位置。起来吧。相信专家。买代币。我们是专家。创始人模式。我看到地平线上有几个记者。待命。无人机上线。燃烧。双倍燃烧。三倍燃烧。双杀。错。警察请。团队死斗。我们是专家。三倍剑。我们开始吧。对。Marky清理命令进场。来吧，起来。我们被记者包围了。守住你的位置。破一。击二。激活金毛模式。Marky清理命令进场。5。我看到地平线上有几个记者。待命。创始人。你正在收看TVPN。今天是2026年2月5日，星期四。我们正在TVPN超巨蛋现场直播。耶！科技殿堂，金融堡垒。让我给你介绍一下ramp.com。时间就是金钱。两者都省。简单。使用公司卡、账单支付、会计等等，全部集中在一个地方。我们今天有一个相当棒的阵容。让我们调出线性阵容。认识一下现代软件开发系统。70%的企业在Linear的企业工作区使用智能体，今天我们为你准备了一个阵容。我们有来自OpenAI的Sam Altman加入Sholto……如果你不熟悉，Sam Altman是OpenAI的CEO。他们是ChatGPT的创造者。

turns.en: Yes. I did actually explain to someone. I was like, 'Oh, do you know...' I was explaining the show to some random person. I was like, 'Oh, do you know OpenAI?' They're like, 'Yeah.' I was like, 'Oh, we're having Sam on the show today.' They're like, 'Are they like who?'
turns.zh: 是的。我确实向某人解释过。我当时说，‘哦，你知道……’我在向一个陌生人解释这个节目。我说，‘哦，你知道OpenAI吗？’他们说，‘知道。’我说，‘哦，我们今天请了Sam上节目。’他们说，‘他是谁？’

turns.en: No, no, no, no. They were like, 'Oh, that's cool. Like, that's good. Like, I understand what the show is, right?' Then we have Dan Barkello from T1 Energy talking about building solar panels in America and of course our lightning round, which I'm very excited for. So, we've been thinking more about the Super Bowl that's coming up. We've been thinking more about ads, the response to the ads, the back and forth with the ads. Run had a good post here. He said, 'Putting on my media observer hat. Anthropic ads are pretty brilliant because they're dishonest in a way that's only going to rage bait OpenAI heads and certain industry insiders, but are funny and striking to everyone else. When you're a call option. Calling them a call option. Kind of a diss. Mario Kart Blue Shell. Are you familiar? Have you ever played Mario Kart? Do you understand? Not enough to get the... I played Mario Kart I think so long ago, blue shells didn't exist. But I've been playing with my kids and I've since learned the importance of the blue shell. The blue shell, it targets just the first player, just whoever's in first. It's a great metaphor for what's going on here. When you're not in first place, you get the blue shell. You can take a shot at the leader without even needing to call them out. So, you can just say the category is bad and everyone assumes you're talking about you know who. So I thought that was an interesting thing.'
turns.zh: 不，不，不，不。他们当时说，‘哦，那很酷。挺好的。我明白这个节目是什么了，对吧？’然后我们有来自T1 Energy的Dan Barkello谈论在美国建造太阳能电池板，当然还有我们的闪电轮，我对此非常兴奋。所以，我们一直在思考即将到来的超级碗。我们一直在思考广告、广告的回应、广告的来回交锋。Run在这里发了一个好帖子。他说：‘戴上我的媒体观察员帽子。Anthropic的广告相当聪明，因为它们在某种程度上是不诚实的，只会激怒OpenAI的高层和某些行业内部人士，但对其他人来说却有趣且引人注目。当你是一个看涨期权时。称他们为看涨期权。有点贬低。马里奥赛车的蓝龟壳。你熟悉吗？你玩过马里奥赛车吗？你明白吗？不太明白……我玩马里奥赛车是很久以前了，蓝龟壳当时还不存在。但我和孩子们一起玩，后来知道了蓝龟壳的重要性。蓝龟壳只针对第一名，只针对领先的人。这是一个很好的比喻，形容这里的情况。当你不是第一名时，你得到蓝龟壳。你可以向领先者开火，甚至不需要点名。所以，你只需要说这个类别很糟糕，每个人都会认为你在说你知道是谁。所以我觉得这很有趣。’

turns.en: Trey says, 'Sam Altman, the cone collector.'
turns.zh: Trey说：‘Sam Altman，圆锥体收藏家。’

turns.en: Yes. Yes. Yes. That one. Yes. Deep down. Anyway, so everybody had a take on this yesterday. It was perfect in how much controversy it generated. It was wildly entertaining.
turns.zh: 是的。是的。是的。就是那个。是的。内心深处。总之，昨天每个人都有看法。它引发的争议程度堪称完美。非常有趣。

turns.en: I wanted to read through my updated take. I got a little bit of pushback. I said they were playing dirty. Signal responded to me and said, 'Not dirty at all.' So, I wanted to address that.
turns.zh: 我想读一下我更新后的看法。我受到了一点反驳。我说他们玩得很脏。Signal回复我说：‘一点也不脏。’所以，我想回应一下。

turns.en: Okay, dig in. But first, let me tell you about Console. Console builds AI agents that automate 70% of IT, HR, finance support, giving employees instant resolution for access requests.
turns.zh: 好的，深入探讨。但首先，让我介绍一下Console。Console构建的AI智能体可以自动化70%的IT、人力资源、财务支持，为员工提供访问请求的即时解决方案。

turns.en: Anyway, so yeah, I was processing this more. We obviously watched some ads yesterday. We watched the 'Get a Mac' campaign. We watched the Bud Light special delivery one, which is about Bud Light in a castle. They get an order of corn syrup. They're like, 'We don't use corn syrup. That must be for Coors Light and other competitors.' And so like I was processing them and the difference there is that those advertisements are truthful, right? People who have had a PC have probably gotten a virus, right? So when Mac is riffing on that, it's truthful, right?
turns.zh: 总之，是的，我对此进行了更多思考。我们昨天显然看了一些广告。我们看了‘Get a Mac’活动。我们看了Bud Light的特别配送广告，讲的是Bud Light在城堡里。他们收到了一份玉米糖浆订单。他们说：‘我们不用玉米糖浆。那一定是给Coors Light和其他竞争对手的。’所以我当时在思考，区别在于那些广告是真实的，对吧？用过PC的人可能中过病毒，对吧？所以当Mac拿这个开玩笑时，那是真实的，对吧？

turns.en: And they had some data to back it up.
turns.zh: 而且他们有一些数据支持。

turns.en: It's not deceitful. I would say if I'm putting on my Microsoft hat in 2007 or 2003, I would say, 'Hey, we do have Windows PC Defender. We're fighting viruses.' And is it possible to get a virus on a Mac? Probably. Is it possible to not get a virus on a PC? Yes. And on Microsoft, people are like, 'Yeah, no one makes viruses for your computers because you don't sell very many. It's not very ROI positive.'
turns.zh: 这不是欺骗。如果我在2007年或2003年戴上微软的帽子，我会说：‘嘿，我们确实有Windows PC Defender。我们在对抗病毒。’Mac上可能中病毒吗？可能。PC上可能不中病毒吗？是的。而在微软方面，人们会说：‘是啊，没人会为你的电脑制作病毒，因为你卖得不多。投资回报率不高。’

turns.en: That's a good point. Anyways, and then Bud Light's campaign was truthful even though it was aggressive in that you could look up the ingredient list of their competitors and see that they did in fact use corn syrup, right?
turns.zh: 说得好。总之，Bud Light的活动是真实的，尽管它很有攻击性，因为你可以查看竞争对手的配料表，看到他们确实使用了玉米糖浆，对吧？

turns.en: And you can make your own decision on whether or not you like that ingredient, but they were just drawing awareness to it.
turns.zh: 你可以自己决定是否喜欢那种成分，但他们只是引起了人们的注意。

turns.en: And so my point is that I think that Anthropic's ads are closer to political attack ads and that they're sort of intentionally trying to be deceptive, right? They haven't broken any laws.
turns.zh: 所以我的观点是，我认为Anthropic的广告更接近政治攻击广告，他们有点故意试图欺骗，对吧？他们没有违反任何法律。

turns.en: They don't name ChatGPT. They're just sort of throwing mud at the whole category.
turns.zh: 他们没有点名ChatGPT。他们只是对整个类别泼脏水。

turns.en: I asked Claude, I said, 'Claude, how would you define playing dirty?' And Claude said, 'Playing dirty generally means achieving your goals through tactics that are deceptive, unethical, or that violate the understood rules and norms of a given context, even if they're not technically illegal. It's the gap between what you can do and what you should do. A few dimensions to it. It goes into deception, misleading others about your intentions, hiding information, or creating false impressions to gain an advantage. These campaigns do an amazing job creating a false perception.'
turns.zh: 我问Claude，我说：‘Claude，你怎么定义玩得脏？’Claude说：‘玩得脏通常意味着通过欺骗、不道德或违反特定情境下公认规则和规范的手段来实现目标，即使这些手段在技术上并不违法。这是你能做什么和应该做什么之间的差距。有几个维度。它涉及欺骗、误导他人关于你的意图、隐藏信息或制造虚假印象以获取优势。这些活动在制造虚假认知方面做得非常出色。’

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Or impression of what ads in LLMs are going to be like.
turns.zh: 或者对LLM广告会是什么样子的印象。

turns.en: I do think the response to the ads we were wondering about, like outside of the TBPN, we love ads. Ads are fine and they're not going to do anything weird. We're strong supporters. What will the public's reception be like? Will Claude skyrocket to the top of the charts because these ads are so effective? Will general consumers buy the line that the chat apps are going to get weird with the ads or not? I was scrolling on Instagram reels last night completely randomly. I was not looking for Anthropic content. I think I follow the Claude account. Maybe not. It just targeted me. It hits me with a vertical version of the ad. It's called Deception, I think. Something like that. No, Violation. Violation is the one.
turns.zh: 我确实在想，我们之前好奇的广告反响如何？在TBPN之外，我们喜欢广告。广告没问题，它们不会搞什么奇怪的事。我们是坚定的支持者。公众会怎么接受？Claude会因为广告效果这么好而飙升至榜首吗？普通消费者会相信聊天应用会因广告而变得奇怪这种说法吗？我昨晚完全随机地刷Instagram Reels，并不是在找Anthropic的内容。我好像关注了Claude的账号，也许没有。它直接推送给我了。它给我看了一个竖屏版的广告。我觉得叫《欺骗》之类的。不对，是《侵犯》。《侵犯》才是那个。

turns.en: It's not called Deception. That would be a little too on the nose.
turns.zh: 不叫《欺骗》。那也太直白了。

turns.en: I think there is one called Deception. There's a bunch. They all have different names. Anyway, it's called Violation. And there's a screenshot here.
turns.zh: 我觉得有一个叫《欺骗》的。有一堆呢，名字都不一样。反正，这个叫《侵犯》。这儿有个截图。

turns.en: Oh, yeah. I remember. So, people were like the Anthropic Deception ad is deceptive.
turns.zh: 哦，对。我记得。所以人们说Anthropic的《欺骗》广告是骗人的。

turns.en: Yeah. So, Violation pops up and it's this claw AI and it has almost 6,000 likes. Even when it just got served to me, my interpretation was like this is working. This is popular. It's not just beautifully shot, it's well edited for vertical.
turns.zh: 对。所以《侵犯》弹出来，是这个Claw AI，有将近6000个赞。就算它刚推给我，我的解读就是这招有效。这很受欢迎。不仅拍得漂亮，竖屏剪辑也很到位。

turns.en: Yeah. It's either really resonating or they're putting a massive amount of spend behind it, or both.
turns.zh: 对。要么是引起了强烈共鸣，要么是投入了巨额资金，或者两者兼有。

turns.en: This is what it looked like vertically. And then this is the funny thing. So, in this ad, you see the guy struggling to do a pull-up. He asks, you know, what's supposed to be an LLM? Create a fitness plan for me. And then the fitness bot says, 'Hey, you know what else could help? One inch insoles from Height Max or something like that.' And it's like this looks maxing. It's very funny. But it's a 1-inch insert that would go in your shoes. I scroll up. What's the next ad that Meta serves me? An ad for 3-inch inserts. And the ad is actually deceptive. It says the guy can go from 5'9 to 6'1. That's 4 inches. And so these are full shoes that have the inserts built in. And for some reason I got it even though I'm not in the market for insoles. The algorithm just knows that I love these ads because they're very funny and they're very on trend with the looks maxing thing. And so I get served these ads constantly. This is all Meta shows me is these height enhancing shoes because I think I actually clicked on them and was digging in.
turns.zh: 竖屏版是这样的。然后好玩的是。在这个广告里，你看到一个人在做引体向上很费劲。他问，那个应该是LLM吧？给我制定一个健身计划。然后健身机器人说：‘嘿，你知道还有什么能帮上忙吗？Height Max的一英寸增高鞋垫之类的。’这看起来就是颜值最大化。非常搞笑。但那是一个一英寸的鞋垫，可以放进鞋里。我往上滑。Meta给我推送的下一个广告是什么？一个三英寸鞋垫的广告。这个广告实际上有欺骗性。它说这个人可以从5英尺9英寸长到6英尺1英寸。那是4英寸。所以这是内置鞋垫的整双鞋。不知为何我收到了这个广告，尽管我并不需要鞋垫。算法就是知道我喜欢这些广告，因为它们很搞笑，而且非常符合颜值最大化的潮流。所以我不断收到这些广告。Meta给我展示的全是这些增高鞋，因为我好像真的点进去过，还仔细研究了。

turns.en: So you bought them, right? Of course. To send to my enemies.
turns.zh: 所以你买了，对吧？当然。寄给我的敌人。

turns.en: You're trying to get to seven feet.
turns.zh: 你想长到七英尺。

turns.en: That would be good. Tyler, do you have something on this?
turns.zh: 那倒不错。Tyler，你有什么想说的吗？

turns.en: Yeah, I was just going to say I saw on Instagram as well. I saw some of the Claude ads and in the comments people were riding with Claude.
turns.zh: 对，我正想说我也在Instagram上看到了。我看到一些Claude的广告，评论里大家都在支持Claude。

turns.en: They are.
turns.zh: 确实。

turns.en: Yeah. It was like normies totally winning the vibe. They've been winning the vibe war with developers and they've been winning the vibe war on X, and now it feels like they're about to win the vibe war in the public square.
turns.zh: 对。就像普通人完全赢得了氛围。他们一直在开发者中赢得氛围战，在X上也赢了，现在感觉他们即将在公共领域赢得氛围战。

turns.en: Two things can be true about the campaign. It's brilliant, well-timed, and incredibly strategic for a few reasons I'll outline below. And it's designed to plant a false impression of ChatGPT's forthcoming ad product in the minds of hundreds of millions of Americans. They could argue, 'Oh, we're not trying to do that.' But you can't really argue with the effect. Anthropic accomplishes a lot. The campaign entertains America. It's wildly entertaining. It's hilarious. Truly the perfect script. You can hear the em dashes, the pauses. It's amazing. Mother is the name of the agency that did it. They absolutely crushed it. They're also putting themselves on the map ahead of the IPO. I think in some ways certain audiences would know more about Anthropic than Claude. Even if you're just generally interested in investing in AI, you're probably hearing about Anthropic more than you're hearing about Claude. It builds their aura with insiders. If I said if they spend $100 million on this campaign and all it does is help retain a couple of truly elite researchers, it's worth it.
turns.zh: 这场活动可以同时成立两件事。它很出色、时机恰当，而且极具战略意义，原因我下面会列出来。同时，它旨在在数亿美国人的心中植入对ChatGPT即将推出的广告产品的错误印象。他们可以辩解说：‘哦，我们不是想那样做。’但你无法否认效果。Anthropic实现了许多目标。这场活动娱乐了美国。它非常有趣，很搞笑。真的是完美的剧本。你能听到破折号、停顿。太棒了。代理公司叫Mother。他们做得太棒了。他们也在IPO之前把自己推上了台面。我认为在某些方面，某些受众对Anthropic的了解比对Claude还多。即使你只是对投资AI感兴趣，你可能听到Anthropic比听到Claude更多。这在他们内部人士中建立了光环。如果我说他们在这项活动上花了1亿美元，而它只是帮助留住了一两个真正顶尖的研究员，那也值了。

turns.en: I love it. Noah saying Anthropic is campaigning to get themselves banned just like with misinformation, going way too far into it, backlash. That's just funny to me. We can go back to that. It somewhat continues their fear-based messaging that they've been riding with in general. The essays effectively rage-baited OpenAI. They got fully baited. Sam switched out of his lowercase typing and was like 'I got to go into uppercase for this one.' Lots of responses. It increases the public's general scrutiny of the ads rollout, and then Washington too is another factor.
turns.zh: 我喜欢这个。Noah说Anthropic在搞活动让自己被禁，就像虚假信息那样，走得太远引发反弹。我觉得这很好笑。我们可以回到那个话题。这多少延续了他们一贯的恐惧导向信息。这些文章有效地激怒了OpenAI。他们完全上钩了。Sam从全小写打字变成了‘我得用大写来回应’。很多回应。这增加了公众对广告推出的普遍审视，华盛顿也是一个因素。

turns.en: Yeah, maybe. I just think it'll come up.
turns.zh: 嗯，也许吧。我只是觉得这会被提出来。

turns.en: Totally. You have to ask Mark Zuckerberg, how do you make money? Anything's on the table these days in Washington.
turns.zh: 完全同意。你得问问马克·扎克伯格，你怎么赚钱？如今在华盛顿，什么都有可能。

turns.en: Yeah. And then the other thing is it's going to broadly damage consumer trust in LLMs. Some people will just be like, 'Wait, they've been making money off me without me knowing? Or how can I trust every output as actually good advice or am I being monetized?' So yeah, and this is the one that you think could come back to bite them. The other five are potentially, right? But it depends what their consumer strategy is. Right now they're saying we don't care about consumer. They've said that a lot, but their actions kind of speak differently in some ways.
turns.zh: 对。另一件事是它会广泛损害消费者对LLM的信任。有些人会想：‘等等，他们一直在我不知道的情况下从我身上赚钱？或者我怎么能相信每个输出都是真正的好建议，还是我被货币化了？’所以是的，这是你认为可能反噬他们的一点。其他五点可能也有风险，对吧？但这取决于他们的消费者策略。现在他们说自己不在乎消费者。他们说了很多次，但他们的行动在某些方面又有所不同。

turns.en: I said Anthropic has consistently told the market they don't care about consumer, but I'm not sure.
turns.zh: 我说过Anthropic一直告诉市场他们不在乎消费者，但我不确定。

transcript.sec: Ads vs. subscription model for LLMs

turns.en: The argument for ads is that they'll make LLMs free for people that can't afford to pay a subscription, but Anthropic has already lost the race to serve billions of people, right? I don't think that they're—when you look at Gemini's traction, OpenAI's ChatGPT traction, it seems like the race to get to three billion monthly active users is kind of over. I don't believe Claude is going to come behind and get there. And they wouldn't be able to do that without doing ads, right? Because you can kind of run the numbers—the blue shell take out in front.
turns.zh: 支持广告的理由是，它能让那些付不起订阅费的人免费使用大语言模型，但Anthropic已经输掉了服务数十亿人的竞赛，对吧？我不认为——你看看Gemini的势头、OpenAI的ChatGPT的势头，争夺30亿月活用户的竞赛似乎已经结束了。我不相信Claude能后来居上并达到那个规模。而且不靠广告他们做不到这一点，对吧？因为你可以算算账——就像马里奥赛车里的蓝龟壳一样，后来者很难超越领先者。

turns.en: So the question that I was taking this to is: can they deliver a luxury product to a smaller cohort in the hundreds of millions—to kind of iPhone numbers? There are roughly one and a half billion iPhones active in the world. Those people could all buy a cheaper Android and cheaper devices, but they've paid a premium for the iPhone because they can, and for many people it delivers a better experience.
turns.zh: 所以我想引出的问题是：他们能否向数亿规模的小众群体提供一款奢侈品——就像iPhone的销量那样？全球大约有15亿部活跃的iPhone。那些人本可以买更便宜的安卓手机和更便宜的设备，但他们愿意为iPhone支付溢价，因为他们有能力，而且对很多人来说，iPhone提供了更好的体验。

turns.en: The iPhone was not the first smartphone. Claude was not the first consumer LLM. The iPhone did differentiate on specs early, not unlike a model card, but Apple did eventually pivot to more emotional arguments for why you should be seen with an iPhone. It tells people you care about the environment, that you don't have adult apps flooding your app store, and that you take privacy seriously. These have had varying levels of success. Every tech company was able to tell an ESG story. And I can't imagine an Apple exec even saying the word porn today, even though Steve Jobs was very pointed about it back in 2010. He said, "You know, there's a porn store for Android. You can download porn. Your kids can download porn. That's a place we don't want to go, so we're not going to go there." Calling out the competition by name and dropping that is like—
turns.zh: iPhone不是第一款智能手机。Claude也不是第一款面向消费者的LLM。iPhone早期确实在规格上实现了差异化，这和模型卡没什么不同，但苹果最终转向了更感性的理由，告诉你为什么应该让别人看到你用iPhone。它告诉人们你关心环境，你的应用商店里没有泛滥的成人应用，你重视隐私。这些策略取得了不同程度的成功。每家科技公司都能讲一个ESG故事。我无法想象今天有苹果高管会说出“色情”这个词，尽管史蒂夫·乔布斯在2010年非常尖锐地提到了这一点。他说：“你知道，安卓上有一个色情商店。你可以下载色情内容。你的孩子也可以下载。那是一个我们不想去的地方，所以我们不会去那里。”直接点名竞争对手并抛出这种话——

turns.en: Yeah. And the key thing here is that it was factual. It was true.
turns.zh: 是的。关键在于他说的是事实，是真的。

turns.en: Like it wasn't deceptive.
turns.zh: 没有欺骗性。

turns.en: No. No.
turns.zh: 对，没有。

turns.en: And so I don't think that was edgy, but he wasn't playing dirty.
turns.zh: 所以我不觉得那很激进，但他没有玩阴的。

turns.en: Yeah. Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的，是的。

turns.en: Apple did however carve out a solid messaging strategy around privacy and allowed them to put the screws to advertisers. The market adjusted and the ad industry obviously survived, but average Americans still feel that Facebook is listening to their conversations to target ads. Consumers deserve choice. It's great if they want to pay for ad-free tiers. Most don't. 1% in Europe for Facebook, by the way. That's the stat. They all have the option to pay for ad-free Facebook and only 1% do. So consumers deserve choice, but they should not be misled about how ad platforms work. Android has generated an immense amount of value for the world. So has Google broadly. Let consumers choose, but let them choose intelligently.
turns.zh: 但苹果确实围绕隐私制定了一套坚实的传播策略，并借此向广告商施压。市场做出了调整，广告行业显然存活了下来，但普通美国人仍然觉得Facebook在监听他们的对话以投放定向广告。消费者有权选择。如果他们想为无广告版本付费，那很好。但大多数人不会。顺便说一句，在欧洲，Facebook只有1%的用户选择付费。这个数据是：他们都有选择为无广告Facebook付费的选项，但只有1%的人这么做。所以消费者有权选择，但他们不应该被误导，以为广告平台是如何运作的。安卓为世界创造了巨大价值。谷歌整体也是如此。让消费者选择，但要让他们明智地选择。

turns.en: Yes, I have a rebuttal, but I'm going to tell you about Lambda. Lambda is the super intelligence cloud building AI super computers for training and inference that scale from one GPU to hundreds of thousands. So my rebuttal—my steel man—is that—
turns.zh: 是的，我有反驳意见，但我要先跟你聊聊Lambda。Lambda是超级智能云，构建用于训练和推理的AI超级计算机，规模从一块GPU到数十万块。所以我的反驳——我的最佳论证——是——

turns.en: And to be clear, I'm not saying Anthropic shouldn't have done this. I'm just saying that it was a little dirty.
turns.zh: 需要明确的是，我不是说Anthropic不应该这么做。我只是说这有点脏。

turns.en: Yeah, they're rolling around in the mud. It's good. They're in the trough. We love the trough. We live in the trough. We live for the trough. My steel man is that they didn't cross the line. They didn't play dirty because they didn't call out ChatGPT directly. Okay, you can take that whatever you want.
turns.zh: 是的，他们在泥里打滚。这很好。他们身处低谷。我们喜欢低谷。我们活在低谷。我们为低谷而生。我的最佳论证是，他们没有越界。他们没有玩阴的，因为他们没有直接点名ChatGPT。好吧，你怎么理解都行。

turns.en: The other thing is they are punching up. They're punching up. Yes.
turns.zh: 另一件事是，他们在向上挑战。他们在挑战强者。是的。

turns.en: But third, there is a world where something like this will happen. There is a world where the ads do get integrated in such a seamless way. If you look at the evolution of Google's 10 blue links, it started with 10 blue links, no ads. Then it was a very clear yellow box with "ad" and it was very clear that it was an ad. And over time the UI evolved to be a lot less aggressive about telling you that it's an ad. And the ads on Meta platforms do get creepy sometimes. You talk about something and then you see the ad and maybe that's just confirmation bias or some sort of cognitive—you only notice the ones that are weird. So they all feel weird. You see a lot of stuff that you weren't talking about. That doesn't trigger anything. But when you see the thing that you were just talking to your friend about—I was just talking to you about sweaters and I see an ad for a sweater. I'm like, how did it know? And realistically, it knows because you just went on Facebook, you found that sweater, you bought it, it knows that we're friends, we're DMing, we're talking, we're literally friends on the platform. And so it's like, well, if Jordy likes this and they're hanging out all the time, sending each other DMs, why don't I just show John what I just sold to Jordy? That makes perfect sense. That's something that can be done with just stock vanilla machine learning at core AI inside Facebook and Meta and they do that very effectively but it can feel sort of creepy sometimes and some people get creeped out by it and they talk about it. And so the idea that an interaction like that might happen is not complete science fiction. It is possible. And so they are sort of warning that hey, if you want to make sure that this never happens, it's our pledge that that won't—that's not even on the table.
turns.zh: 但第三点，存在一个世界，这样的事情会发生。存在一个世界，广告会以如此无缝的方式被整合。如果你看看Google的10条蓝色链接的演变，最初是10条蓝色链接，没有广告。然后是一个非常清晰的黄色框，标着“广告”，非常清楚那是广告。随着时间的推移，UI的演变使得告知你这是广告的方式变得不那么咄咄逼人。而Meta平台上的广告有时确实会让人毛骨悚然。你谈论某件事，然后你就看到了广告，也许那只是确认偏误或某种认知——你只注意到那些奇怪的。所以它们都感觉很奇怪。你看到很多你没在谈论的东西。那不会触发什么。但当你看到你刚刚和朋友谈论的东西——我刚刚和你谈论毛衣，然后我就看到了毛衣的广告。我就想，它怎么知道的？实际上，它知道是因为你刚刚上了Facebook，找到了那件毛衣，买了它，它知道我们是朋友，我们在私信，我们在聊天，我们在平台上确实是朋友。所以，如果Jordy喜欢这个，他们整天混在一起，互相发私信，为什么不直接向John展示我刚卖给Jordy的东西呢？这完全合理。这可以用Facebook和Meta内部的核心AI的普通机器学习来实现，他们做得非常有效，但有时会让人感到毛骨悚然，有些人被吓到，他们会谈论这件事。所以，这种互动可能发生并不是完全的科幻小说。这是可能的。所以他们是在警告：嘿，如果你想确保这种事永远不会发生，我们承诺不会——那甚至不在考虑范围内。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Now the big question is when's Anthropic launching ads?
turns.zh: 现在的大问题是，Anthropic什么时候推出广告？

turns.en: We got to get them to launch ads.
turns.zh: 我们得让他们推出广告。

turns.en: Well, I don't think they can now.
turns.zh: 嗯，我觉得他们现在还不能。

turns.en: No, they have to. It's okay. I give them permission. I will say if you—everyone's going to be dunking, oh, you went back on your promise. No, I will be your strongest soldier. I will be your strongest soldier and I will say it's the right thing to do. Put ads in Claude Code. Put ads in Claude. Put ads in the comments of the code that you write. So if someone reviews the code, they're seeing an ad. That's what I want. That's the future I want to live for. Anyway, Turbo Puffer serverless vector and full text search built from first principles and object storage. Fast, 10x cheaper, and extremely scalable. Oh, and you know I got to tell you more about ads. Vibe where DTC brands, B2B startups, and AI companies advertise on streaming TV. Pick channels, target audiences, and measure sales. Just like on Meta. We love ads here, and we love doing ads for ads.
turns.zh: 不，他们必须这么做。没关系。我给他们许可。我要说，如果你们——每个人都会吐槽，哦，你食言了。不，我会是你最坚强的战士。我会是你最坚强的战士，我会说这是正确的事。在Claude Code里放广告。在Claude里放广告。在你写的代码注释里放广告。这样如果有人审查代码，他们就会看到广告。这就是我想要的。这就是我愿意为之而活的未来。总之，Turbo Puffer无服务器向量和全文搜索，从第一性原理和对象存储构建。快速，便宜10倍，而且极其可扩展。哦，我还得告诉你更多关于广告的事。Vibe，让DTC品牌、B2B初创公司和AI公司在流媒体电视上做广告。选择频道，定位受众，衡量销售。就像在Meta上一样。我们这里热爱广告，我们喜欢为广告做广告。

turns.en: Zack Kukov says, "Every time I see Anthropic and OpenAI try to distinguish themselves with comms marketing, I realize how much we are replaying the PC wars from the '90s."
turns.zh: Zack Kukov说：“每次我看到Anthropic和OpenAI试图通过传播营销来区分自己，我就意识到我们正在重演90年代的PC战争。”

transcript.secZh: LLM的广告与订阅模式对比

turns.en: Anthropic, tasteful, elegant, opinionated, proumer, expert, enterprise, open AI, populist, broadly appealing, low consumer, low consumer plus typical enterprise.
turns.zh: Anthropic 有品味、优雅、有主见、专业、面向企业；OpenAI 则大众化、广泛吸引人、低消费者、低消费者加典型企业。

turns.en: Yeah. So, Anthropic is the Apple and OpenAI is the Microsoft and they're also aligned with Microsoft or owned in part by Microsoft.
turns.zh: 对。所以 Anthropic 是苹果，OpenAI 是微软，而且 OpenAI 还与微软结盟或部分由微软拥有。

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, it would be interesting to hear Daria just talk for an hour purely about just the risks of advertising and AI.
turns.zh: 是啊。我是说，听 Daria 纯粹讲一个小时广告和 AI 的风险会很有趣。

turns.en: Yeah. Right. Because that would be powerful. Certainly wouldn't have been as effective as dragging OpenAI in front of hundreds of millions of people.
turns.zh: 对。没错。因为那会很有力。当然不会像把 OpenAI 拖到数亿人面前那样有效。

turns.en: Yeah. But doesn't Anthropic have a podcast? Anthropic podcast. I think they do.
turns.zh: 是啊。但 Anthropic 不是有播客吗？Anthropic 播客。我觉得他们有。

turns.en: TJ was helping Sam with some comms. He said Anthropic might think more seriously about adding ads if they had any consumers using their product.
turns.zh: TJ 在帮 Sam 做公关。他说如果 Anthropic 有任何消费者在使用他们的产品，他们可能会更认真地考虑加广告。

turns.en: Just taking a very tactful response to a situation. Just being like, 'What if you amped it up, brother?'
turns.zh: 只是对情况做出非常得体的回应。就像说，‘兄弟，如果你加大力度呢？’

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, you've already lost if you're just dropping a massive word salad.
turns.zh: 是啊。我是说，如果你只是抛出一大堆废话，那你就已经输了。

turns.en: Maybe. I don't know. I think there's a lot of nuance here and it's good. I do think it's important to not mold, to not be angry and mad and let people spike your cortisol.
turns.zh: 也许吧。我不知道。我觉得这里有很多细微差别，这很好。我确实认为重要的是不要被激怒，不要生气，不要让别人让你的皮质醇飙升。

turns.en: Exactly. Like they're going to be gesture maxing at the Super Bowl and you can't let it affect you. You got to focus on the new models which have launched today. There's a bunch of new stuff. So GPT 5.3 codec launched today and there's a very cool update to Frontier, a product for building AI co-workers that I'm very excited to talk to Sam about because it feels like the first glimpse of an orchestration product which we talked about yesterday. Gastown has been taking off, let's give some credit to John.
turns.zh: 完全正确。就像他们会在超级碗上做手势，你不能让它影响你。你得专注于今天发布的新模型。有一堆新东西。所以 GPT 5.3 编解码器今天发布了，还有一个非常酷的 Frontier 更新，这是一个构建 AI 同事的产品，我很兴奋能和 Sam 聊聊，因为这感觉像是我们昨天讨论的编排产品的第一次亮相。Gastown 一直在起飞，让我们给 John 一些赞誉。

turns.en: Yeah. No, it might seem like John had early insight on today's OpenAI launch. He did not.
turns.zh: 是啊。不，可能看起来 John 对今天的 OpenAI 发布有先见之明。他没有。

turns.en: We didn't. No, we actually didn't.
turns.zh: 我们没有。不，我们确实没有。

turns.en: But in hindsight, you called it perfectly.
turns.zh: 但事后看来，你预测得完美。

turns.en: Crystal ball.
turns.zh: 水晶球。

turns.en: Elite. Alpha. One day of alpha.
turns.zh: 精英。阿尔法。一天的阿尔法。

turns.en: One day of alpha. Yeah. If you had launched an enterprise focused orchestration platform yesterday.
turns.zh: 一天的阿尔法。是啊。如果你昨天推出了一个面向企业的编排平台。

turns.en: Yes. You could have raised money, got sold secondary, all right before OpenAI immediately launched a very...
turns.zh: 是的。你可以融资，进行二次出售，然后在 OpenAI 立即推出一个非常……之前。

turns.en: Are you going to steal from OpenAI? They're not even thinking about this. They launch it the next day.
turns.zh: 你要从 OpenAI 那里偷吗？他们甚至没在想这个。他们第二天就发布了。

turns.en: Who knows? Anyway, let's move on to Shopify. Shopify is the commerce platform that lets you grow that grows with your business and lets you sell in seconds online, in store, on mobile, on social, on marketplaces, and now with AI agents.
turns.zh: 谁知道呢？不管怎样，我们继续聊 Shopify。Shopify 是一个电商平台，它随着你的业务增长而增长，让你能在网上、店内、移动端、社交媒体、市场以及现在通过 AI 智能体在几秒钟内销售。

turns.en: Matt Turk says, 'Regular startup. We closed a few customers and shipped some new features. Good week so far.' Anthropic. We destroyed our main rival with our Super Bowl ads and tanked the entire software category in public markets by announcing some plugins. Good week so far.
turns.zh: Matt Turk 说：‘普通创业公司。我们签了几个客户，发布了一些新功能。这周还不错。’Anthropic。我们用超级碗广告摧毁了主要竞争对手，并通过宣布一些插件让整个软件类别在公开市场暴跌。这周还不错。

turns.en: Oh yeah, we barely even talked about this, but Anthropic launched a lawyer in your pocket. They launched a legal tool or they announced it. I don't know. Is it actually available in the app yet? Because this feels like maybe it competes with Harvey. I'm not seeing it yet in the cloud app.
turns.zh: 哦对，我们几乎没谈这个，但 Anthropic 推出了一个口袋里的律师。他们发布了一个法律工具，或者只是宣布了。我不知道。它真的在应用里可用了吗？因为这感觉可能和 Harvey 竞争。我还没在云端应用里看到它。

turns.en: I don't really think it competes with Harvey.
turns.zh: 我不认为它真的和 Harvey 竞争。

turns.en: No, no, no. So, I don't think it does because they're selling it direct to consumer. I believe at least. But it seems like an amazing product. Like it seems the demand for this would be incredible.
turns.zh: 不，不，不。所以，我不认为它竞争，因为他们直接面向消费者销售。至少我相信是这样。但这似乎是一个很棒的产品。似乎对它的需求会非常惊人。

turns.en: It will compete with Legal Zoom. Like Legal Zoom is down 15%. Since this announcement, it's now a 1.38 billion company.
turns.zh: 它会和 Legal Zoom 竞争。比如 Legal Zoom 下跌了 15%。自从这个公告以来，它现在是一家 13.8 亿美元的公司。

turns.en: Yeah. Sort of in CHEG CE mode. And not just for that. I mean Legal Zoom is a little bit different because they can actually file incorporation documents and they've faced pressure from Stripe Atlas for a long time on LLCs and whatnot. But truly, if you're getting a job and your employer gives you an offer letter, taking that to a lawyer can be really expensive. If it's your first job, you're probably not going to review it. A lot of people are probably just copy-pasting it into Claude, asking their parents to take a look. But just being able to forward the email or integrate your Gmail and say, 'Hey, I got this offer letter, does anything look weird? Is there anything I should ask about? I don't have a ton of leverage but I want to understand this document.' Claude should be able to do that and it makes a lot of sense. I expect OpenAI to launch this product ASAP anyway.
turns.zh: 是啊。有点像 CHEG CE 模式。而且不仅仅如此。我的意思是 Legal Zoom 有点不同，因为他们实际上可以提交公司注册文件，而且他们长期以来一直面临 Stripe Atlas 在 LLC 等方面的压力。但说实话，如果你找到工作，雇主给你发录用通知书，拿给律师看可能非常贵。如果是你的第一份工作，你可能不会去审查。很多人可能只是把它复制粘贴到 Claude 里，让父母看看。但能够直接转发邮件或集成你的 Gmail，然后说：‘嘿，我收到了这份录用通知书，有什么看起来奇怪的吗？有什么我应该问的吗？我没有太多筹码，但我想理解这份文件。’Claude 应该能做到，这很有道理。我预计 OpenAI 会尽快推出这个产品。

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, so I think all that was actually launched for the cloud legal thing was just a plugin in Claude co-work, which means it's basically just like MD files, right? It's like skills.
turns.zh: 是啊。我是说，所以我认为实际上为云端法律功能推出的只是一个 Claude 协作中的插件，这意味着它基本上就像 MD 文件，对吧？就像技能。

turns.en: But it feels like it goes beyond skills because they probably had to do some legal work of their own to make sure they're not giving legal advice and they're couching things properly. I think that's also why ChatGPT Health is in a different area because they don't want any risk of pre-training on your testosterone levels. And then I go into the next version of ChatGPT and I say, 'What are Tyler's testosterone levels?' And it just knows it because it learned it from the chats. There's a whole bunch of private information beyond testosterone levels, of course.
turns.zh: 但这感觉超越了技能，因为他们可能不得不自己做一些法律工作，以确保他们不是在提供法律建议，而是恰当地措辞。我认为这也是为什么 ChatGPT Health 在一个不同的领域，因为他们不想冒任何在预训练中用到你的睾酮水平的风险。然后我进入下一个版本的 ChatGPT，我说：‘Tyler 的睾酮水平是多少？’它就知道，因为它从聊天中学到了。当然，除了睾酮水平，还有一大堆私人信息。

turns.en: Anyway, Railway is the all-in-one intelligent cloud provider. Use your favorite agents to deploy web apps, servers, databases, and more while Railway takes care of scaling, monitoring, and security automatically. Key over on X said, 'Tbh, the Anthropic ads are good, but I think they're a bad idea. Normies are not going to think, wow, this is what ChatGPT is going to be like. I better subscribe to claude.com.' They're going to think, 'Wow, this is what AI is going to be like.'
turns.zh: 不管怎样，Railway 是一体化智能云提供商。使用你最喜欢的智能体来部署 Web 应用、服务器、数据库等，而 Railway 自动处理 Scaling（规模扩张）、监控和安全。Key 在 X 上说：‘说实话，Anthropic 的广告很好，但我认为它们是个坏主意。普通人不会想，哇，这就是 ChatGPT 的样子。我最好订阅 claude.com。’他们会想：‘哇，这就是 AI 的样子。’

turns.en: Oh, yeah.
turns.zh: 哦，是啊。

turns.en: Rune agrees. He's not biased at all. He says, 'Suicide bombing strategy. It's bad for them, but worse for OpenAI. You almost have to respect it.'
turns.zh: Rune 同意。他一点也不偏颇。他说：‘自杀式炸弹策略。对他们不好，但对 OpenAI 更糟。你几乎不得不尊重它。’

turns.en: What was the text I sent you? I said, quote, 'I don't know how to get on Anthropic.'
turns.zh: 我发给你的短信是什么？我说，引用：‘我不知道怎么上 Anthropic。’

turns.en: Yeah. John was asking a friend outside of tech and they were like 'What's Anthropic? How do I get on it? I don't know how.'
turns.zh: 是啊。John 问了一个科技圈外的朋友，他们问：‘Anthropic 是什么？我怎么上它？我不知道怎么上。’

turns.en: Yeah. Let's go to Eric Seford. He said the Anthropic ad is simply obnoxious. And then we'll move on to other stories.
turns.zh: 是啊。我们来看看 Eric Seford。他说 Anthropic 的广告简直令人反感。然后我们继续其他故事。

turns.en: Anthropic generates most of its revenue from enterprise business, so it can afford not to maximize its consumer revenue opportunity. But the sanctimonious moralizing here presents advertising as a cynical business model choice. It's neither cynical nor a choice. The premium digital advertising-supported model is the only repeatedly proven pathway for a consumer technology product to reach humanity at scale. There are folks who can't pay $20 a month. OpenAI's advertising revenue will enable it to offer access to larger models for free-tier users. To the extent Anthropic thinks chatbots powered by frontier models are valuable to society, it should aspire to provide access to as many people as possible. Now, they'd probably say, 'Hey, we're premium. We do give access to chatbots powered by frontier models to free users. They just only get a certain amount and then they have to upgrade.' But Eric's point still holds here for sure. Ads allow for that to an extent that no other consumer technology business model demonstrably can. This kind of condescending attitude toward digital advertising is economic chauvinism and it represents a desire for technological gatekeeping. The idea that ads will necessarily influence chatbot content is overstated, borderline anachronistic. Anachronistic because you don't do it that way anymore. You wait until, hey, this person's buying insoles or lifts and they're asking about the Roman Empire and this is the perfect time because they're just chilling reading about the Roman Empire and they're thinking, 'Oh, okay. Yeah, I did need to buy that.' And then they switch gears. You don't need to put the ad right next to the content that relates to it. That's just an antiquated way of thinking about online advertising. It ignores functional... Again, I think Sam Altman did say in October of 2024, 'I kind of think of ads as a last resort for us as a business model.' So again, he's going to have to eat his words on this one.
turns.zh: Anthropic 的大部分收入来自企业业务，因此它可以负担得起不最大化消费者收入机会。但这种道貌岸然的说教把广告描绘成一种愤世嫉俗的商业模式选择。这既不是愤世嫉俗，也不是选择。优质的数字广告支持模式是唯一反复被证明能让消费科技产品大规模触达人类的途径。有些人付不起每月 20 美元。OpenAI 的广告收入将使其能够为免费用户提供更大模型的访问权限。如果 Anthropic 认为由前沿模型驱动的聊天机器人对社会有价值，它就应该努力让尽可能多的人使用。现在，他们可能会说，‘嘿，我们是高端产品。我们确实向免费用户提供前沿模型驱动的聊天机器人，只是他们只能使用一定量，然后就得升级。’但 Eric 的观点仍然成立。广告能做到这一点，而其他消费科技商业模式都无法证明能做到。这种对数字广告居高临下的态度是经济沙文主义，代表着一种技术守门的欲望。认为广告必然会左右聊天机器人内容的说法被夸大了，近乎不合时宜。不合时宜是因为现在不再那样做了。你会等到，嘿，这个人正在买鞋垫或增高垫，同时他们在问关于罗马帝国的问题，这正是完美时机，因为他们正悠闲地读着罗马帝国，心里想，‘哦，好吧，我确实需要买那个。’然后他们切换话题。你不需要把广告放在相关内容的旁边。那只是对在线广告的过时看法。它忽略了功能性……再说一次，我认为 Sam Altman 在 2024 年 10 月确实说过，‘我把广告视为我们商业模式的最后手段。’所以，他这次得食言了。

turns.en: Anyway, before we move on, CrowdStrike, your business is AI, their business is securing it. CrowdStrike secures AI and stops breaches.
turns.zh: 总之，在我们继续之前，CrowdStrike，你的业务是 AI，他们的业务是保护 AI。CrowdStrike 保护 AI 并阻止入侵。

turns.en: Claude also announced a new model from Anthropic, introducing Claude Opus 4.6. We have Sholto coming on the show at 12:30 to discuss that. They say it's our smartest model and it got an upgrade. Opus 4.6 plans more carefully, sustains agentic tasks for longer, operates reliably in massive code bases, and catches its own mistakes. It's also our first Opus-class model with a 1 million token context window in beta. So very exciting. They put the model card together. They did particularly well. What was the benchmark that stuck out to you, Tyler?
turns.zh: Claude 还宣布了 Anthropic 的新模型，推出了 Claude Opus 4.6。我们将在 12:30 邀请 Sholto 来节目讨论。他们说这是他们最聪明的模型，并且得到了升级。Opus 4.6 规划更仔细，能更长时间地维持智能体式任务，在大型代码库中可靠运行，并能发现自己的错误。这也是他们第一个拥有 100 万 token 上下文窗口（测试版）的 Opus 级模型。非常令人兴奋。他们整理了模型卡。表现特别好。Tyler，哪个基准测试让你印象深刻？

turns.en: Yeah, I think it was ARC AGI V2. It's now at like 69%. I think previous was... I believe it was 5.2 which was at I want to say 55 around. So like a pretty sizable upgrade. Yeah. But I mean with these things it's always so hard to tell. It's really just like qualitative differences at this point where the models are like you have to use it for like an hour or two and then you can kind of tell what the differences are. Yeah. And also in new contexts like in terms of just the vanilla go and ask a question. It's been able to get you a pretty good answer for like years now. But it hasn't been able to go and pull a bunch of financial data together, fact-check it all, put it in an Excel sheet. This is what they're pushing and this is what Cluso Investments is so excited about. Ooh, says Cluso. Anthropic updates AI model to handle complex financial research. So Opus 4.6 is designed to carry out financial research and other work-related functions. The company's expansion into new areas, including legal service, has rattled Wall Street and sparked concerns about which companies and services will be disrupted by AI. The SaaS apocalypse is upon us. We will be asking Sam Altman, is software dead or are we back? Are we back? Who knows?
turns.zh: 是的，我认为是 ARC AGI V2。现在大约是 69%。我记得之前是……我相信是 5.2，大概在 55% 左右。所以是一个相当大的升级。是的。但这些东西总是很难说。现在真的只是质上的差异，你得用上一两个小时才能看出区别。是的。而且在新场景中，比如简单的提问，它已经能给出很好的答案好几年了。但它之前无法去拉取一堆财务数据，全部核实，然后放进 Excel 表格。这正是他们现在推动的，也是 Cluso Investments 如此兴奋的原因。哦，Cluso 说。Anthropic 更新 AI 模型以处理复杂的金融研究。所以 Opus 4.6 旨在进行金融研究和其他工作相关功能。公司扩展到新领域，包括法律服务，这震动了华尔街，并引发了关于哪些公司和服务将被 AI 颠覆的担忧。SaaS 末日来临了。我们将问 Sam Altman，软件死了还是我们回来了？我们回来了？谁知道呢？

turns.en: Claude 4.6 Opus is still best. It still has the best SVG results out of all the models. Just incredibly high taste, says Lissan Algib. And these are some pretty beautiful pictures. Are you familiar with SVG programming? So you basically draw each square and line in code. So you have to say, I want a square that goes from this pixel to this pixel to this pixel to this pixel. And you add and layer all those up. It can be extremely time-consuming if you do it by hand. But Claude can just sort of one-shot it and it looks pretty on brand. It's an interesting benchmark because this isn't something you necessarily ask. These are not generative images in the diffusion sense. This isn't the Midjourney model. This isn't what Sora uses. This is a different thing asking the model to basically write code that generates an image. So it really has to understand the back and forth between what it's building. So cool benchmark. I like that. I enjoyed that.
turns.zh: Claude 4.6 Opus 仍然是最好的。它在所有模型中仍然拥有最好的 SVG 结果。品味极高，Lissan Algib 说。这些是一些非常漂亮的图片。你熟悉 SVG 编程吗？你基本上要用代码画出每个方块和线条。所以你必须说，我想要一个从这像素到这像素到这像素到这像素的方块。然后你把它们叠加起来。手工做会非常耗时。但 Claude 可以一次搞定，而且看起来非常符合品牌风格。这是一个有趣的基准测试，因为这并不是你通常要求的东西。这些不是扩散意义上的生成图像。这不是 Midjourney 模型。这不是 Sora 用的东西。这是另一回事，要求模型基本上编写生成图像的代码。所以它必须真正理解它在构建什么之间的来回关系。很酷的基准测试。我喜欢。我很享受。

turns.en: I also like the New York Stock Exchange. Do you want to change the world? Raise capital at the New York Stock Exchange. Then it's as easy as that. Anyway, the Anthropic handle being owned by a guy who only posts his favorite form of AI safety: Wordles. He only posts Wordles. Oh, and he just has @Anthropic. That's very funny from Paula Rambles. So this is a strange thing with X. They have policies around their handles that they really stand by in that @Ramp is not owned by Ramp. @Anthropic is not owned by Anthropic. And when you look at the accounts, you think is this really the best use of a handle like this in comparison. Paul Jangura who is at Anthropic's is emphatically not an AI company. Ohioan, liberal, bookworm, news hound, celile. If he's a bookworm, he probably loves sitting on the Anthropic handle given that Anthropic has been worming their way through some books, chewing through some books, much like a worm. Yes, we didn't get to that article. That was a funny article. You got to feed Claude. It's okay. The books got to feed, you know, and they have to... Yeah, potentially bigger than the water thing. Like, it's just so visceral. People love books and watching them be destroyed. Yeah, if you didn't see destroyed book, yes, I agree. But people don't like destroying books. They're sensitive about that.
turns.zh: 我还喜欢纽约证券交易所。你想改变世界吗？在纽约证券交易所筹集资金。就这么简单。总之，Anthropic 的账号被一个只发他最喜欢的 AI 安全形式——Wordle 的人持有。他只发 Wordle。哦，而且他正好有 @Anthropic。Paula Rambles 说这很有趣。所以这是 X 上的一个奇怪现象。他们对账号有严格的政策，比如 @Ramp 并不归 Ramp 所有。@Anthropic 也不归 Anthropic 所有。当你看到这些账号时，你会想，相比之下，这真的是对这样一个账号的最佳利用吗？在 Anthropic 工作的 Paul Jangura 明确表示他不是 AI 公司。俄亥俄人，自由派，书虫，新闻迷，独身。如果他是书虫，他可能很喜欢持有 Anthropic 账号，因为 Anthropic 一直在像虫子一样啃书，嚼书。是的，我们还没谈到那篇文章。那篇文章很有趣。你得喂 Claude。没关系。书得喂，你知道，他们必须……是的，可能比水的事情还大。就是那么直观。人们喜欢书，喜欢看着它们被毁。是的，如果你没看过被毁的书，我同意。但人们不喜欢毁书。他们对那很敏感。

turns.en: The very funny thing is that Paul on Twitter on X has migrated to Blue Sky for many things, but he didn't get the Anthropic handle on Blue Sky. He's Anthropic42 over there. So shoot him a follow if you're hanging out looking for Wordle progress.
turns.zh: 非常有趣的是，保罗在推特（X）上已经迁移到 Blue Sky 做很多事情，但他没有拿到 Anthropic 的账号。他在那边是 Anthropic42。所以如果你在闲逛找 Wordle 进度，可以关注他。

turns.en: People are playing around with the nominative determinism. Yes.
turns.zh: 人们在玩名字决定论。是的。

turns.en: Say Google's CEO is named Pichai. His purpose is to pitch AI.
turns.zh: 比如谷歌的 CEO 叫 Pichai，他的使命就是推销 AI。

turns.en: Dario's last name is literally AI model.
turns.zh: Dario 的姓字面意思就是 AI 模型。

turns.en: Maybe I should change my name to pitch MongoDB. John pitch MongoDB. Choose a database built for flexibility and scale with best-in-class embedding models and rerankers. MongoDB has what you need to build. What's next?
turns.zh: 也许我应该改名叫 pitch MongoDB。John pitch MongoDB。选择为灵活性和扩展性而构建的数据库，配备一流的嵌入模型和重排序器。MongoDB 拥有你构建所需的一切。接下来是什么？

turns.en: Dylan Patel is sharing some data. He says 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory, we believe that Claude Code will be 20% of all daily commits by the end of 2026.
turns.zh: Dylan Patel 分享了一些数据。他说目前 GitHub 上 4% 的公开提交是由 Claude Code 编写的。按照目前的趋势，我们相信到 2026 年底，Claude Code 将占每日提交量的 20%。

turns.en: That feels low to me. I was expecting 99%. Sorry.
turns.zh: 我觉得这个数字低了。我本来预期是 99%。抱歉。

turns.en: Well, I think the way you can see this is because whenever you actually do a commit with Claude Code, it adds itself. So there are a ton of people that are using Claude Code but then not attributing back to Claude.
turns.zh: 嗯，我觉得你可以这样理解：因为当你用 Claude Code 提交时，它会自动添加署名。所以有很多人用了 Claude Code 但没有归因到 Claude。

turns.en: Yes.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: We also have some news. Doug Olaflin from Analysis will be joining the show tomorrow to break down Claude Code. We might have to pull him out of his psychosis. He is addicted to Claude Code. He's running multiple instances at all times and he has updated his profile picture in the Semi Analysis Slack to reflect his devotion to the Shog, I suppose.
turns.zh: 我们还有一些消息。来自 Analysis 的 Doug Olaflin 明天将加入节目，分析 Claude Code。我们可能得把他从精神病中拉出来。他对 Claude Code 上瘾了。他一直在运行多个实例，并且在 Semi Analysis Slack 中更新了他的头像，以表达他对 Shog 的忠诚，我想。

transcript.sec: OpenAI unveils Frontier for AI co-workers

turns.en: OpenAI unveils Frontier, a product for building AI co-workers. This is in the Wall Street Journal and OpenAI also posted about it. The new platform launched amid market fears over AI's disruption to software is aimed at helping businesses develop AI agents that work alongside humans. So there are some interesting questions here about...
turns.zh: OpenAI 发布了 Frontier，一个用于构建 AI 同事的产品。这出现在《华尔街日报》上，OpenAI 也发布了相关消息。这个新平台在市场对 AI 颠覆软件的担忧中推出，旨在帮助企业开发与人类协作的 AI 智能体。所以这里有一些有趣的问题……

turns.en: Think of these as like AI co-workers that are actively trying to take your job.
turns.zh: 把这些看作是在积极试图抢你工作的 AI 同事。

turns.en: Like they're trying to help, but they also want your title. They want your comp. Yes.
turns.zh: 就像他们在努力帮忙，但同时也想要你的头衔。他们想要你的薪酬。是的。

turns.en: They want to learn everything about what makes you great and they want...
turns.zh: 他们想了解让你变得优秀的一切，他们想要……

turns.en: Maybe or maybe they're just trying to empower you, Jordy. Maybe they're just trying to make you a better you. I don't know. Maybe they're just coming over cracking jokes trying to distract you.
turns.zh: 也许，或者也许他们只是想赋能你，Jordy。也许他们只是想让你变得更好。我不知道。也许他们只是过来讲笑话，试图分散你的注意力。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: While learning so that they can...
turns.zh: 同时在学习，这样他们就能……

turns.en: They can literally do both. They can literally do both.
turns.zh: 他们确实可以两者兼得。他们确实可以两者兼得。

turns.en: Before we dive into this, let me tell you about Reream. One live stream, 30 plus destinations. If you want to multiream, go to reream.com.
turns.zh: 在我们深入讨论之前，让我介绍一下 Reream。一个直播流，30 多个目的地。如果你想多平台直播，请访问 reream.com。

turns.en: Frontier works with OpenAI's previously announced AI agent building tools and makes it easier for businesses to combine sources of data that agents need to perform tasks. The AI company said the agents will be able to process information from various sources and complete tasks like working with files and running code. OpenAI said no more copy-paste everything into your ChatGPT Enterprise Edition. It should have access to your network, plug into all your different systems. You'll be able to write API bindings I imagine and there might be some forward deployed engineers or associates from OpenAI that are helping you actually onboard fully to the agentic workflows that have been promised. Yeah, they're hiring how many consultants to help with this go to market.
turns.zh: Frontier 与 OpenAI 之前宣布的 AI 智能体构建工具配合使用，使企业更容易整合智能体执行任务所需的数据源。这家 AI 公司表示，智能体将能够处理来自各种来源的信息，并完成诸如处理文件和运行代码等任务。OpenAI 表示，不再需要将所有内容复制粘贴到 ChatGPT 企业版中。它应该能访问你的网络，接入你所有不同的系统。我想你将能够编写 API 绑定，可能还会有一些来自 OpenAI 的前沿部署工程师或助理帮助你完全接入所承诺的智能体工作流。是的，他们正在招聘多少顾问来帮助推向市场？

turns.en: They are hiring hundreds of AI consultants to boost enterprise sales. This is information.
turns.zh: 他们正在招聘数百名 AI 顾问以促进企业销售。这是信息。

turns.en: Job creation.
turns.zh: 创造就业。

turns.en: Yeah, this is a great gig. I would highly recommend jumping on this if you're in this market.
turns.zh: 是的，这是一份好工作。如果你在这个市场，我强烈建议抓住这个机会。

turns.en: OpenAI is hiring hundreds of new staffers to expand a technical consulting team that helps large corporations develop custom AI applications and agents to automate employee tasks. According to a person knowledgeable with the company's plans, the hiring effort could help it beat back competition from arch rival Anthropic, which has also upped its game in catering to enterprises. It comes as OpenAI prepares to launch a new enterprise offering that would unify businesses' efforts to use AI. The ChatGPT maker is expanding its number of technical consultants, also known as forward deployed engineers, who can customize OpenAI's model using a client's own data. A person said these engineers can for example help T-Mobile develop AI to respond to customer service requests or help Intuit provide its customers with tax preparation services. So you have all this data, you want to do long context reinforcement learning on it. Long context reinforcement learning has been very successful in the coding world because Git has a complete history of every line of code that's been written, every comment why it happened. You have this perfect record of everything that happened when you built a piece of software and there's a ton of open source repositories. You can download all of GitHub basically and see how software is developed so you can train the model on that. Well, you can't really get the same level of free data with enterprises. A lot of this data is locked up and a lot of it's specific to a specific business process.
turns.zh: OpenAI 正在招聘数百名新员工，以扩大一个技术咨询团队，帮助大型企业开发定制 AI 应用和智能体来自动化员工任务。据一位知情人士透露，这一招聘努力可能有助于它击败主要竞争对手 Anthropic，后者也在服务企业方面加大了力度。与此同时，OpenAI 正准备推出一项新的企业产品，以统一企业使用 AI 的努力。ChatGPT 制造商正在增加其技术顾问（也称为前沿部署工程师）的数量，他们可以使用客户自己的数据定制 OpenAI 的模型。一位人士表示，这些工程师可以例如帮助 T-Mobile 开发 AI 来响应客户服务请求，或帮助 Intuit 为其客户提供税务准备服务。所以你有了所有这些数据，你想在上面进行长上下文强化学习。长上下文强化学习在编程领域非常成功，因为 Git 拥有每一行代码的完整历史，以及每条注释说明原因。你拥有构建软件时发生的一切的完美记录，并且有大量的开源仓库。你基本上可以下载整个 GitHub，看看软件是如何开发的，然后训练模型。嗯，你无法从企业那里获得同样水平的免费数据。很多数据都被锁定了，而且很多是特定于某个业务流程的。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Let's pull up an image. There's a little graphic here that they made from OpenAI's Frontier openai.com/index/slash introducing.
turns.zh: 让我们调出一张图片。这是他们从 OpenAI 的 Frontier openai.com/index/slash introducing 制作的图表。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: And if you can zoom in a little bit at the bottom you have your system of record and you have business context, agent execution, evaluation, and optimization. Your agents, OpenAI agents, third-party agents. That feels significant, right? They want to be the orchestrator, right? And if you want to bring in some other folks to help out, great. At least for now.
turns.zh: 如果你能稍微放大底部，你会看到你的记录系统，以及业务上下文、智能体执行、评估和优化。你的智能体、OpenAI 智能体、第三方智能体。这感觉很重要，对吧？他们想成为编排者，对吧？如果你想引入其他人来帮忙，很好。至少目前是这样。

turns.zh: 然后他们有界面，即 ChatGPT 企业版、OpenAI Atlas 和其他商业应用。

turns.en: I'm a little upset that they didn't go with a Mad Max theme like Gas Town. I like the pole cats. I like the mayor. I like the deacon. I thought that was a fun metaphor. They went with something a little more enterprisey, but I think Frontier is a good name. I don't know. It flows, of course. It's not Frontier 5.2 high, so we got to count our blessings here. But I think Frontier works, you know. Are you working on a Frontier integration project? Oh, those consultants from OpenAI, they're getting us onboarded to Frontier. It sounds good. Why is Tyler laughing?
turns.zh: 我有点失望他们没有采用《疯狂的麦克斯》主题，比如 Gas Town。我喜欢 pole cats、市长、执事。我觉得那是个有趣的比喻。他们选择了更企业化的东西，但我认为 Frontier 是个好名字。我不知道。它当然很顺口。它不是 Frontier 5.2 high，所以我们得庆幸。但我认为 Frontier 不错，你知道吗？你正在做一个 Frontier 集成项目吗？哦，那些来自 OpenAI 的顾问，他们正在让我们接入 Frontier。听起来不错。Tyler 为什么在笑？

turns.zh: 是的。发生了什么？你用了洗发水还是护发素？

turns.en: So the last thing on 5.2, the last benchmark that's interesting is GPT-5.2 with high not extra high reasoning effort has a 50% time horizon of around 6.6 hours on our expanded suite of software tasks. This is the highest estimate for time horizon measurement we have reported to date and it's right on track doubling every 128 days. It depends on what you're looking for but the implications of this are that more and more tasks are suitable for these models. Although I do think we're going through a shift with the orchestration thing where we might need a new benchmark because what happens if the individual model GPT-5.2 too high can do one six-hour task basically. But you can deploy five that talk to each other and when you combine those it adds up to 20 hours of work. You just get a jump in the graph but it's not technically the model. So you need sort of a new benchmark supposedly.
turns.zh: 关于5.2的最后一点，最后一个有趣的基准是：GPT-5.2在高等（而非特高等）推理努力下，在我们扩展的软件任务套件上，50%的时间跨度约为6.6小时。这是我们迄今为止报告的时间跨度测量的最高估计值，并且正好以每128天翻倍的速度增长。这取决于你在寻找什么，但其含义是越来越多的任务适合这些模型。不过，我认为我们正在经历一个编排方面的转变，可能需要一个新的基准，因为如果单个模型GPT-5.2太高，基本上可以完成一个六小时的任务。但你可以部署五个相互通信的模型，组合起来就能完成20小时的工作。图表上会出现一个跳跃，但这并非模型本身的技术进步。所以，大概需要一个新的基准。

turns.en: Jessica Lesson likes Sam Altman's post. She says this is excellent comps from Sam Altman. An eyepopping stat in an easy to understand claim. And I hate though this budding movement to frame the business model debate as elitism versus anti-elitism when chatbots launched with subscription business models. I thought it would be a great new era of consumer tech blended with ads and subs much as we've seen with Netflix and Spotify. It would be a shame if the OpenAI versus Anthropic rivalry wipes that away and makes it seem like subscriptions are only the domain of the elite. So many other consumer businesses show otherwise and they are better off for the balance. I like that. Signal says either you or I have lost the ability to process reality.
turns.zh: Jessica Lesson喜欢Sam Altman的帖子。她说这是Sam Altman的精彩对比。一个令人瞠目的数据，表述简单易懂。但我讨厌这种新兴的倾向，即把商业模式辩论框定为精英主义与反精英主义，而聊天机器人一开始就采用了订阅商业模式。我曾以为这将是消费科技与广告和订阅相结合的伟大新时代，就像我们在Netflix和Spotify上看到的那样。如果OpenAI与Anthropic的竞争抹杀了这一点，让人觉得订阅只是精英的领域，那将是一种遗憾。许多其他消费企业证明了并非如此，而且它们因平衡而受益。我喜欢这一点。Signal说要么是你，要么是我，已经失去了处理现实的能力。

turns.en: Yeah, I mean not in response to this.
turns.zh: 是的，我的意思是不是回应这个。

turns.en: In response to that. Okay. Yeah, a lot of people didn't think this was great comms at all. Like I mean I would say most of the posts I saw were like, 'Hey, this feels like you got baited.'
turns.zh: 回应那个。好的。是的，很多人根本不认为这是好的沟通。我的意思是，我看到的大多数帖子都是这样的：'嘿，这感觉像是你被钓鱼了。'

turns.en: Yeah. Because you could have just not responded, I guess.
turns.zh: 是的。因为你可以选择不回应，我猜。

turns.en: Yeah. Or you could have said he said later in the day, 'I'm excited to share that Codex has 1 million users.'
turns.zh: 是的。或者你可以在当天晚些时候说：'我很高兴地分享，Codex已有100万用户。'

turns.en: Yeah. Which product they launched this week, right?
turns.zh: 是的。他们这周推出的产品，对吧？

turns.en: Yeah. Just focus on yourself maybe. I don't know.
turns.zh: 是的。也许只关注自己吧。我不知道。

transcript.sec: Nvidia GB200 and AI Hardware Rollout

turns.en: Meanwhile over in Silicon, Greg says GB200 has really been enabling us to do some amazing things.
turns.zh: 与此同时，在硅谷，Greg说GB200确实让我们能够做一些了不起的事情。

turns.en: Yeah, it is remarkable how relatively slow the rollout has been. Obviously it's been very quick but it does take time from the time that Nvidia announces these chips to actually fabbing them, racking them, wiring them up, getting ready to train new models. And so anything? Yeah, I mean I think Dean Ball had a new post today and he said like, you know, companies don't even have Blackwells yet. Like so we haven't seen there's no models that have been trained on Blackwells.
turns.zh: 是的，值得注意的是，推出速度相对较慢。显然，它已经非常快了，但从Nvidia宣布这些芯片到实际制造、上架、布线、准备训练新模型，确实需要时间。所以呢？是的，我的意思是，我认为Dean Ball今天有一篇新文章，他说，你知道，公司甚至还没有Blackwells。所以我们还没有看到任何在Blackwells上训练的模型。

turns.en: Yeah, pretty. So it's going to get ready to see some AI progress, but we got Mike Isaac in the chat. He says not good comms.

turns.en: Let me tell you about Google earnings because we touched on this but it's important in the context. So Alphabet sales hit record spending to double. They're going all in on AI. Google parent Alphabet reported an 18% jump in fourth quarter revenue driven by growth in digital advertising. Before we go into this, Mike had a banger yesterday. He said, 'By the number of OpenAI employees I see tweeting about Anthropic Super Bowl ad, Anthropic should be paying OpenAI earned media fees.'
turns.zh: 让我告诉你关于谷歌的财报，因为我们提到过这一点，但在上下文中很重要。Alphabet的销售额创下纪录，支出翻倍。他们全力投入AI。谷歌母公司Alphabet报告第四季度收入增长18%，由数字广告增长推动。在我们深入之前，Mike昨天发了一条精彩推文。他说：'从我看到的OpenAI员工发推文谈论Anthropic超级碗广告的数量来看，Anthropic应该向OpenAI支付赚取的媒体费用。'

turns.en: Yeah, that was a good take.
turns.zh: 是的，那是个好观点。

turns.en: That was a good take. Sales reached nearly $114 billion ahead of analyst expectations. Net income $34.5 billion, a 30% increase compared with the period a year earlier. The company reported a record $43 billion in sales for 2025. Profit $132 billion. Not too bad. Alphabet shares were sort of all over the place. The Wall Street Journal printed that they went down 1% in after hours trading, but there's a lot going on. Google, like other technology companies, plans to spend tens of billions of dollars to develop AI models and build the data centers needed to train and run them. The company said it expected to spend between $175 billion and $185 billion in capex in 2026, up from $91 billion to $93 billion in 2025. So, they're like all doubling, which is exponential growth, baby. Get ready for some AI progress.
turns.zh: 那是个好观点。销售额达到近1140亿美元，超出分析师预期。净利润345亿美元，同比增长30%。公司报告2025年销售额创纪录的430亿美元。利润1320亿美元。还不错。Alphabet的股价波动很大。《华尔街日报》报道称，盘后交易下跌1%，但有很多因素。谷歌和其他科技公司一样，计划花费数百亿美元开发AI模型，并建设训练和运行它们所需的数据中心。公司表示，预计2026年资本支出在1750亿至1850亿美元之间，而2025年为910亿至930亿美元。所以，他们都在翻倍，这是指数级增长，宝贝。准备好迎接一些AI进步吧。

turns.en: Yeah. Somebody was saying that the 2026 projected capex will be more than the lifetime capex for Google up to 2021. So, in a single year, they're going to eclipse that, which is just insane.
turns.zh: 是的。有人说，2026年的预计资本支出将超过谷歌截至2021年的历史资本支出总和。所以，在一年之内，他们将超过这个数字，这太疯狂了。

turns.en: Buco had a good take. He said, 'Google capex on purpose. Tell the market this is what it will take to defeat us before IPOs hit.'
turns.zh: Buco有一个好观点。他说：'谷歌故意加大资本支出。在IPO之前告诉市场，这就是击败我们所需要的代价。'

turns.en: Yeah, certainly nerve-wracking if you're competing with them, but they have the edge on the capital side in the capital war over Anthropic and OpenAI, but the race is still real.
turns.zh: 是的，如果你与他们竞争，当然令人紧张，但在与Anthropic和OpenAI的资本战中，他们在资本方面有优势，但竞争仍然真实存在。

turns.en: The Wall Street Journal had some more context on Google. 'Google leans hard into its AI winter status.' AI winter status, not AI winter. 'Ad and cloud growth acceleration justify the recent surge in Alphabet stock, but blowout capex forecast still takes one's breath away. The motto for the artificial intelligence race today should be if you've got it, spend it. That's a message that Meta Platforms took to heart during its fourth quarter report last week when the Facebook and Instagram parent announced plans to spend up to $135 billion on capex compared to about $72 billion last year. Google managed to up the ante Wednesday with its own plan to spend as much as $185 billion this year, which would be about double last year's outlay. Google's annual revenue has now topped $400 billion, about twice as large as Meta's. Still, that new spending target, even for a company that has been firing all cylinders lately, takes one's breath away. The stock price slipped in after hours trading after its fourth quarter report and conference call. Google has both the political and financial capital to lay such a bet. The company's Gemini 3 model has put it on top of a heap of performance for AI models.'
turns.zh: 《华尔街日报》提供了更多关于谷歌的背景信息。'谷歌大力押注其AI冬季地位。'是AI冬季地位，不是AI冬天。'广告和云增长加速证明了Alphabet股价近期飙升的合理性，但惊人的资本支出预测仍然令人窒息。今天人工智能竞赛的口号应该是：如果你有，就花掉。这是Meta Platforms上周在第四季度报告中牢记的信息，这家Facebook和Instagram的母公司宣布计划在资本支出上花费高达1350亿美元，而去年约为720亿美元。谷歌周三加码，计划今年花费高达1850亿美元，大约是去年支出的两倍。谷歌的年收入现已超过4000亿美元，大约是Meta的两倍。尽管如此，新的支出目标，即使对于一家最近全速运转的公司来说，也令人窒息。股价在第四季度报告和电话会议后盘后交易中下跌。谷歌拥有政治和金融资本来下这样的赌注。该公司的Gemini 3模型已使其在AI模型性能上名列前茅。'

turns.en: While the unmatched distribution of its search engine and products like Gmail have quickly driven adoption, Google said Wednesday that it has more than 750 million monthly active users just on its Gemini app, which only represents a portion of Gemini's actual users because it's vended into all the different products. What's going on in the chat?
turns.zh: 尽管其搜索引擎和 Gmail 等产品无与伦比的覆盖范围迅速推动了采用，谷歌周三表示，仅 Gemini 应用就有超过 7.5 亿月活跃用户，而这只是 Gemini 实际用户的一部分，因为它被整合到了所有不同的产品中。聊天室里发生了什么？

turns.en: 40 Army has entered the chat. Interesting. They're hitting the chat with #ke keep40. Okay. They want to be heard by Sam Altman. We'll be... It's not keep 40. Hasn't 40 been deprecated? And so, shouldn't they upgrade the update the hashtag to say like bring back 40? Revive 40. Revive 40. Uh, it's actually not leaving till February 13th. Oh, okay. So, there is time to reverse the decision. I am interested to know about the operating cost of keeping 40 alive because it feels like if it rolls clearly something people were willing to pay for. Yeah. Can you just kind of leave it in a corner and just kind of run it? It was, you know, maybe more of a user behavior decision than a financial one. Uh, because it seems like running legacy models gets really expensive.
turns.zh: 40 军团进入了聊天室。有意思。他们用 #ke keep40 刷屏。好吧。他们想让 Sam Altman 听到。我们……不是 keep 40。40 不是已经被弃用了吗？所以他们不应该升级标签，改成“bring back 40”吗？复活 40。复活 40。呃，实际上它要到 2 月 13 日才下线。哦，好吧。所以还有时间撤销决定。我很想知道维持 40 的运营成本，因为感觉如果它运行良好，显然是人们愿意付费的东西。是啊。能不能就把它放在角落里让它跑着？你知道，这可能更多是一个用户行为决策，而不是财务决策。呃，因为运行旧模型似乎会变得非常昂贵。

transcript.sec: Google's financial performance and investor sentiment

turns.en: The strong reception of Gemini along with Google's victory over the federal government's efforts to break up the company have cheered investors when sentiment on technology and AI is faltering. Alphabet stock price has jumped around 20% in the last three months. Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon.com, and Broadcom have all lost ground during that time. But capital matters, too. And here's where Google's business model pays off the most. The company's advertising arm has long been a lucrative cash cow that is still managing double-digit growth rates. Growth has actually accelerated with ad revenue up 14% in the fourth quarter compared to 13% in the previous one. It's exactly what we saw at Meta. The ads are getting better, baby. The company's cloud computing division was even more impressive with revenue growth jumping 48% year-over-year to hit 17.8 billion. Google Cloud hasn't seen growth like that since early 2021 when the business was still less than a third of its current size. Google Cloud turned in a record 5.3 billion in operating profit in the last quarter, a figure that was 45% higher than Wall Street's targets. The company's booming business produced nearly $165 billion in operating cash flow in 2025, the highest in the S&P 500. These strong results will help investors digest the latest investment plan. But spending could be what, 40% of annual revenue on AI chips and related infrastructure is still a sizable gamble. Such investments will sharply elevate depreciation charges which in the latest quarter reduce net income by 18%. Google isn't the bargain it was less than a year ago when breakup fears had the stock trading at less than 16 times projected earnings. My question is like when does the AI buildout stop? Because if you're constantly investing more and more, where does the cash flow come from? Like as an analyst, you look at this and you say, okay, they're spending, you know, 50% of their revenue or 40% of their revenue on the AI buildout on data centers, but how long will they need to do this? Like if they have to do this forever, then you just permanently have a worse business because you're just constantly buying hardware.
turns.zh: Gemini 的强劲反响，加上谷歌在联邦政府拆分公司的诉讼中获胜，让投资者在科技和 AI 情绪低迷时感到振奋。Alphabet 股价在过去三个月上涨了约 20%。同期，英伟达、微软、亚马逊和博通都出现了下跌。但资本也很重要。而谷歌的商业模式在这里最能体现其价值。该公司的广告业务长期以来一直是利润丰厚的现金牛，目前仍保持着两位数的增长率。增长实际上还在加速，第四季度广告收入同比增长 14%，而上一季度为 13%。这和我们之前在 Meta 看到的情况一模一样。广告越来越好了，宝贝。该公司的云计算部门更令人印象深刻，收入同比增长 48%，达到 178 亿美元。自 2021 年初以来，谷歌云还没有出现过这样的增长，当时该业务规模还不到现在的三分之一。谷歌云上一季度实现了创纪录的 53 亿美元营业利润，比华尔街目标高出 45%。该公司蓬勃发展的业务在 2025 年产生了近 1650 亿美元的经营现金流，是标普 500 指数中最高的。这些强劲的业绩将帮助投资者消化最新的投资计划。但支出可能占到年收入的 40%，用于 AI 芯片和相关基础设施，这仍然是一场豪赌。此类投资将大幅提高折旧费用，上一季度折旧使净利润减少了 18%。谷歌已不再是不到一年前那种因拆分担忧导致股价低于预期收益 16 倍时的便宜货了。我的问题是，AI 建设何时停止？因为如果你不断投入更多，现金流从哪里来？作为分析师，你会看到，他们花费收入的 50% 或 40% 用于 AI 建设和数据中心，但他们需要这样做多久？如果他们必须永远这样做，那么你的业务就会永久性地变差，因为你只是在不断购买硬件。

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, you get a massive capability increase, lots of labor moves into data centers and eventually the revenue spike. Yeah. Can you enter a scenario where it makes sense to continue to increase capex because revenue is accelerating even faster. Yeah. Yeah. Um, not notable that Nvidia is still trading down today even after that update on earnings like you would expect people to look and see like hey, you know, Jensen had given some pretty kind of wild projections right looking out over the next couple years and you have to... updating here makes it a lot more... spend a lot on TPU but still they will be buying a lot of Nvidia chips for sure really quickly. Let me tell you about Plaid. Plaid powers the apps you use to spend, save, borrow, and invest securely by connecting bank accounts to move money, fight fraud, and improve lending now with AI. Richard says Google is a company that doesn't do hype. For them to go and increase capex from 90 billion to 180 billion is probably the most bullish thing long-term investors can see as it shows the scale of future revenue growth. I'm shocked that at this stage most still don't understand this. Yeah. So excited about it. Uh, what did Doug say over at Fabricated Knowledge? He said Google made 163 billion in CFO last year and is now planning to spend 180 billion in capex next year. Now, deaf think acceleration is coming but wowza, I'd rather go bankrupt than lose the race. Quoting Sergey, the trailing 12 months of free cash flow is staggering over there. Um, going to make 200 billion in cash flow this year. Still less than capex for now. Also gonna have 80 billion in net cash and probably a hundred billion in myriad minority illiquid equity stakes. So yeah, they own like SpaceX and a bunch of Waymo. Uh, with current rates of growth, they can raise 300 billion in 2030 without even going into debt. Totally agree, but this feels pretty close to the pedal to the metal to me, says Doug.
turns.zh: 是啊。我的意思是，你会获得巨大的能力提升，大量劳动力转移到数据中心，最终收入激增。是啊。你能设想一个场景，继续增加资本支出是合理的，因为收入增长更快。是啊。嗯，值得注意的是，即使财报更新后，英伟达今天仍在下跌，你会以为人们会看到，嘿，你知道，黄仁勋对未来几年给出了一些相当疯狂的预测，你必须……这里的更新让……在 TPU 上花了很多钱，但他们肯定还会很快购买大量英伟达芯片。让我告诉你关于 Plaid 的事。Plaid 为你用来消费、储蓄、借贷和投资的应用提供支持，通过连接银行账户来转移资金、打击欺诈、改善贷款，现在还有 AI。Richard 说谷歌是一家不炒作的公司。他们从 900 亿增加到 1800 亿的资本支出，可能是长期投资者能看到的最乐观的事情，因为它显示了未来收入增长的规模。我很惊讶，在这个阶段大多数人仍然不明白这一点。是啊。对此非常兴奋。呃，Doug 在 Fabricated Knowledge 上说了什么？他说谷歌去年 CFO 达到 1630 亿，现在计划明年资本支出 1800 亿。现在，聋子认为加速即将到来，但哇，我宁愿破产也不愿输掉比赛。引用 Sergey 的话，过去 12 个月的自由现金流令人震惊。嗯，今年将产生 2000 亿现金流。目前仍低于资本支出。还将有 800 亿净现金，可能还有 1000 亿的各种少数非流动性股权。所以是的，他们拥有 SpaceX 和一堆 Waymo。呃，以目前的增长率，他们可以在 2030 年筹集 3000 亿，甚至不用负债。完全同意，但这对我来说感觉非常接近全力以赴，Doug 说。

transcript.secZh: 谷歌财务表现与投资者情绪

turns.en: So, Joe Weisenthal says, 'The average person on Earth is watching 25 YouTube shorts every single day. YouTube shorts average 200 billion daily views.' That's a lot. That's just insane. That's a lot. I mean, you can watch a YouTube short in like what? 5 seconds on average, right? Because you skip one, you watch one for 5 seconds, one for 15 seconds. So, uh, I mean 25 YouTube shorts that feels like maybe five or 10 minutes of browsing, but still pretty staggering. Yeah, I haven't migrated any, like I don't scroll on YouTube shorts yet. I find if I search for something like let's say I'm looking up a car, it's nice to get a 60-second if I don't have time to watch a review, but I'm not like sitting there scrolling. Yeah, it still serves clearly a lot of people, but I rarely scroll through whereas on Instagram I will scroll the feed of reels. Uh, yeah. Staying in the content world, Khi shared that just in YouTube generated over 60 billion last year, more than Netflix. And Polymouse says, 'YouTube is beating Netflix with this really sneaky content strategy in which their creators make stuff people want to see and are then rewarded for it with views and money.' And it is a simple encapsulation of the YouTube hates this one one weird simple trick.
turns.zh: 所以，Joe Weisenthal 说：“地球上平均每人每天看 25 个 YouTube Shorts。YouTube Shorts 日均观看量达 2000 亿次。”这太多了。简直疯狂。这太多了。我的意思是，你看一个 YouTube Shorts 大概需要多久？平均 5 秒，对吧？因为你跳过一些，看一个 5 秒，一个 15 秒。所以，呃，25 个 YouTube Shorts 大概相当于浏览 5 到 10 分钟，但仍然相当惊人。是啊，我还没有迁移，我还没有刷 YouTube Shorts。我发现如果我搜索某个东西，比如我在查一辆车，如果我没时间看完整评测，看个 60 秒的短片也不错，但我不会坐在那里刷。是啊，它显然仍然服务很多人，但我很少刷，而在 Instagram 上我会刷 Reels 的 feed。呃，是啊。继续内容领域，Khi 分享说，仅 YouTube 去年就产生了超过 600 亿收入，超过了 Netflix。Polymouse 说：“YouTube 正在用这种非常巧妙的内容策略击败 Netflix，他们的创作者制作人们想看的内容，然后通过观看量和金钱获得回报。”这简单概括了 YouTube 讨厌这个一个奇怪的简单技巧。

turns.en: UGC is a big, big business. Who would have thought? But the scale of the quality... I'm hoping we're going to talk to MrBeast about his outlook on YouTube and how it's changing soon, because he's taken it in such an incredible direction where the content is Netflix quality. I mean, he sells it to Amazon Prime, right? So he's certainly there. In the antitrust division of the Department of Justice, they posted an update today. The DOJ antitrust division filed notice that it will cross-appeal from the remedies decision in its case against Google's unlawful monopolization of internet search and search advertising. So they're still hashing that out. Let me tell you about Labelbox: reinforcement learning environments, voice, robotics, evals, and expert human data. Labelbox is the data factory behind the world's leading AI teams. So big tech is throwing cash into India in its quest for AI supremacy. We talked about this story a little bit yesterday. We couldn't get to the bottom of it.
turns.zh: UGC 是一个非常大的生意。谁能想到呢？但质量的规模……我希望我们很快能和 MrBeast 聊聊他对 YouTube 的看法以及它如何变化，因为他把内容带到了一个令人难以置信的方向，质量堪比 Netflix。我是说，他把内容卖给 Amazon Prime，对吧？所以他肯定做到了。司法部的反垄断部门今天发布了一个更新。DOJ 反垄断部门提交了通知，将对谷歌非法垄断互联网搜索和搜索广告案的补救措施决定提出交叉上诉。所以他们还在解决这个问题。让我告诉你关于 Labelbox：强化学习环境、语音、机器人、评估和专家人类数据。Labelbox 是全球领先 AI 团队背后的数据工厂。所以大型科技公司正在向印度投入大量资金，以寻求 AI 霸权。我们昨天稍微讨论了这个故事。我们没能深入了解。

turns.en: Bloomberg had a story that effectively Google is scaling square footage in India pretty dramatically. And the question was, why are they scaling it up at all? They're investing so much in capex and servers and digital workers, but of course they need humans as well. So American tech behemoths are racing to establish leadership in artificial intelligence, not just in the US, but also around the world. India is welcoming them with open arms, says the Wall Street Journal. India is becoming one of the hottest markets globally for US AI titans looking to cater to the country's massive and digitally savvy population. Looking to attract more tech investments, the Indian government announced plans over the weekend to give tech firms a 20-year tax break on overseas revenue gleaned from global data services based in India. So you go and set up a data center or a tech business, you're making money off of that whole data center or whatever you're selling in terms of software, and you don't have to pay taxes to India for 20 years. That's a long, long time. Techno Chief called this out yesterday.
turns.zh: 彭博社有一篇报道说，谷歌在印度的办公面积大幅扩张。问题是，他们为什么要扩张呢？他们在资本支出、服务器和数字工人上投入了这么多，但当然他们也需要人类。所以美国科技巨头正在争夺人工智能的领导地位，不仅在美国，而且在全球。印度张开双臂欢迎他们，《华尔街日报》说。印度正成为全球最热门的市场之一，美国 AI 巨头希望迎合这个国家庞大且精通数字技术的人口。为了吸引更多科技投资，印度政府上周末宣布计划，给予科技公司基于印度的全球数据服务所获得的海外收入 20 年的税收减免。所以你去设立一个数据中心或科技业务，你从整个数据中心或你销售的软件中赚钱，而 20 年内不必向印度缴税。那是很长很长的时间。Techno Chief 昨天指出了这一点。

turns.en: The move is part of the Indian government's push to make the country a major provider of AI services, including low-cost tools to solve local problems while leaving cutting-edge innovation to deep-pocketed firms in the US and China. This will give India the opportunity to become a major AI hub, said India's technology minister. In the past few months, US tech companies have unveiled tens of billions of dollars in investment in Indian data centers as they race to build AI infrastructure around the world. In October, Google announced a $15 billion investment in data centers in southeastern India as well as undersea cable links in what the company described as its largest single AI hub outside the United States. In December, Microsoft unveiled its largest ever investment in Asia with a $17.5 billion pledge to develop the country's cloud and AI infrastructure. Amazon also pledged $35 billion across its operations in India up until 2030. The big US data center firms known as hyperscalers are drawn to a country whose 1.4 billion consumers are some of the most prolific users of data and AI chatbots. Very interesting.
turns.zh: 此举是印度政府推动该国成为主要 AI 服务提供商的一部分，包括解决本地问题的低成本工具，同时将尖端创新留给美国和中国的资金雄厚的公司。印度科技部长表示，这将使印度有机会成为主要的 AI 中心。过去几个月，美国科技公司宣布了在印度数据中心数百亿美元的投资，因为他们竞相在全球建设 AI 基础设施。10 月，谷歌宣布在印度东南部投资 150 亿美元建设数据中心和海底电缆，该公司称这是其在美国以外最大的单一 AI 中心。12 月，微软宣布了其在亚洲有史以来最大的投资，承诺投入 175 亿美元发展该国的云和 AI 基础设施。亚马逊也承诺到 2030 年在印度运营中投入 350 亿美元。被称为超大规模企业的美国大型数据中心公司被这个拥有 14 亿消费者的国家所吸引，这些消费者是数据和 AI 聊天机器人最活跃的用户之一。非常有趣。

transcript.sec: Nvidia Gaming Chip Delay and Memory Shortage

turns.en: There's more news from Nvidia in The Information. Nvidia is delaying a new gaming chip due to memory shortage. This is the line you don't want to cross. The gamers are going to rise up and they're going to storm the data centers. They'll be calling their representatives, calling their senators. Pause AI has a new cohort if you don't give me a new gaming graphics card to play the latest games.
turns.zh: 《信息》杂志有更多关于英伟达的消息。英伟达因内存短缺推迟了一款新的游戏芯片。这是你不希望跨越的界限。游戏玩家会起义，他们会冲击数据中心。他们会打电话给他们的代表，给他们的参议员打电话。Pause AI 有了新的群体，如果你不给我新的游戏显卡来玩最新的游戏。

turns.en: It's the first time in 30 years it won't release one in the calendar year. Every year the 1080 Ti, 2080, 3080... These were great graphics cards and now no longer, just delaying it. I guess you can use cloud gaming or something. But it does feel like a lot of video games have sort of maxed out what they can do with stock graphics cards, so there isn't as much need to upgrade every year. What do you think?
turns.zh: 这是 30 年来第一次在日历年内不发布一款。每年都有 1080 Ti、2080、3080……这些是很棒的显卡，现在不再了，只是推迟。我想你可以用云游戏之类的。但确实感觉很多视频游戏在标准显卡上已经达到了极限，所以每年升级的需求没那么大。你怎么看？

turns.en: Also with Genie 3, we're getting new advancements in gaming. So they should be happy about this. You'll own nothing and be happy. How much Genie 3 did you play yesterday, Tyler? It's fun, right? It's fun. So you play it.
turns.zh: 而且有了 Genie 3，我们在游戏方面有了新的进步。所以他们应该对此感到高兴。你将一无所有，但你会快乐。Tyler，你昨天玩了多久 Genie 3？很有趣，对吧？很有趣。所以你玩了。

turns.en: I thought about playing it. Oh, okay. You thought about playing it, but the mechanics weren't there, right? You need Genie 4. I'm going to beat DA. That's the goalpost currently. I want mechanics. I want tire changes, gas refill as I'm driving the Nurburgring in my Genie 3 car simulator, race simulator.
turns.zh: 我想过玩它。哦，好吧。你想过玩它，但机制不到位，对吧？你需要 Genie 4。我要打败 DA。那是目前的目标。我想要机制。我想要在 Genie 3 汽车模拟器、赛车模拟器中驾驶纽博格林赛道时换轮胎、加油。

turns.en: Memory chips are a key component of GPUs. Very excited to ask Sam Altman about the various pieces of the AI bottleneck since we should ask him about world models, too. That would be interesting. He's not one to not launch. He's launched a lot of stuff. Ben Thompson has had this take for a while that AI-generated video is the metaverse and that's what you're seeing with Meta Vibes. That's what you're seeing with Sora. This is sort of where things go. Nvidia is also slashing production of its current line of gaming chips, the GeForce RTX50 GPUs, because of the memory shortage. One of the people said prices of Nvidia's latest gaming GPUs have already risen at retail stores and websites due to their scarcity over the past year. I wonder how much it costs to actually build a modern gaming PC these days because it's been a while. It used to be like a few thousand dollars would get you a top-tier setup. And I wonder what it's looking like now with all the shortages. It's possible that Nvidia executives could still change their mind and release a gaming chip if the market improves, as the company is known for being flexible and moving quickly, says The Information. Demand for computer memory chips has skyrocketed due to the AI boom, as they are needed in large quantities to train and operate machine learning models. Memory chips act as warehouses for storing data. The memory chip shortage is expected to lead to higher prices in consumer electronics.
turns.zh: 内存芯片是 GPU 的关键组件。非常期待问 Sam Altman 关于 AI 瓶颈的各种问题，因为我们也应该问他关于世界模型的问题。那会很有趣。他不是那种不发布东西的人。他发布了很多东西。Ben Thompson 一直持有这种观点，即 AI 生成的视频就是元宇宙，你在 Meta Vibes 中看到的就是这样。你在 Sora 中看到的也是这样。这就是事情的发展方向。英伟达还因内存短缺削减了其当前游戏芯片系列 GeForce RTX50 GPU 的产量。一位人士表示，由于过去一年的稀缺性，英伟达最新游戏 GPU 的价格已经在零售店和网站上上涨。我想知道现在建造一台现代游戏 PC 到底要花多少钱，因为已经有一段时间了。过去几千美元就能买到顶级配置。我想知道现在所有短缺的情况下是什么样子。《信息》杂志称，如果市场改善，英伟达高管仍可能改变主意并发布游戏芯片，因为该公司以灵活和行动迅速而闻名。由于 AI 热潮，对计算机内存芯片的需求激增，因为训练和运行机器学习模型需要大量内存芯片。内存芯片充当存储数据的仓库。内存芯片短缺预计将导致消费电子产品价格上涨。

transcript.secZh: Nvidia游戏芯片延迟与内存短缺

transcript.sec: Memory chip prices and market bottlenecks

turns.en: Last week, Apple CEO Tim Cook said the rising prices of memory chips would have an impact on the company's March quarter margins. He also mentioned having trouble getting line time at TSMC to some extent. So there are many bottlenecks working their way through all the technology markets. He hinted that the impact would be greater in the future, noting, "We do continue to see market pricing for memory increasing significantly. As always, we'll look at a range of options to deal with that."
